source.definition: to gather mature crops or fruits from the fields or plants

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "harvest" originates from Old English "hærfest", which referred to the season of gathering crops and later came to mean the act of collecting ripe produce.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „harvest“ provine din engleza veche „hærfest“, care desemna sezonul de recoltare a culturilor și ulterior a căpătat sensul de a aduna produsele coapte.

source.additional: Regular verb, commonly used in agricultural contexts.

target.definition: a culege fructele sau plantele coapte

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a culege" derives from the Latin "colligere", meaning "to gather together, to collect".
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a culege“ provine din latină, de la „colligere“, care înseamnă „a aduna, a strânge“.

target.additional: Regular first conjugation verb (-a). Frequently used in agricultural contexts.

examples.sourceText: We are doing a gathering of plants.

explanation.sourceText: As in English: for the harvest of ripe fruits.
explanation.targetText: La fel ca în germană: pentru recoltarea fructelor coapte.

examples.sourceText: I am looking forward to the harvest.
I harvest the apples in the garden.
Do you harvest the vegetables?
She harvests the flowers in spring.
We harvest a lot of fruit this year.
examples.targetText: Noi culesc în acest an multe fructe.

examples.sourceText: Do you harvest the berries?
They harvest the cultivated plants.
I successfully completed the wheat harvest.
examples.targetText: Am încheiat cu succes culesul grâului.

examples.sourceText: They picked the strawberries.
He harvested a large amount of vegetables every summer.
examples.targetText: El culegea o cantitate mare de legume în fiecare vară.

examples.sourceText: I had already harvested the grapes when it started to rain.
examples.targetText: Culesesem deja strugurii când a început să plouă.

examples.sourceText: I will harvest the fruit next week.
They will harvest the cultivated plants tomorrow.
examples.targetText: Ea o să culeagă plantele agricole mâine.

examples.sourceText: I would harvest the nuts in autumn if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș culege nucile toamna, dacă aș avea timp.

examples.sourceText: It would be good if you finish the harvest today.
examples.targetText: Ar fi bine să finalizezi culesul astăzi.

examples.sourceText: Harvest the fruits in time!
Please harvest the potatoes!
The harvested apples are fresh.
While harvesting the grapes, I learn a lot about harvest time.
examples.targetText: Culegând strugurii, învăț multe despre perioada de recoltare.

learningTips.text: Remember 'harvest' sounds like 'hard vest'—imagine wearing a hard vest while gathering crops to protect yourself.
The Romanian 'a culege' sounds like 'cool edge'—think of the cool edge of a knife used to pick fruits carefully.
Link 'a culege' with places where you gather fruits and vegetables, like farms or gardens, to fix its meaning.
Think of 'harvest' as the rewarding end of the growing season, when all the hard work pays off by collecting ripe produce.
